description: Uses statistical methods to spot unusual patterns in your data -- a sudden spike in transactions, an unexpected drop in records, or values way outside normal ranges. Can also monitor data volumes to catch unexpected spikes or drops that signal upstream problems.
name: Anomaly Detection

paramsSchema.description: Pipeline-working-storage backend. Resolved at codegen and deploy time via storage_backends (tenant_id, environment, backend) lookup. Default inherits from pipeline.default_storage_backend when set.
paramsSchema.name: storage_backend

usageGuidance.when_to_use: Statistical anomaly detection on column values and volumes

description: Compiles all data quality metrics into a single scorecard -- an overall health grade for your data. Published to the data catalog so stakeholders can see at a glance whether they can trust the data.
name: DQ Scorecard Publish

paramsSchema.description: Where to publish the DQ scorecard
paramsSchema.name: publish_target

paramsSchema.description: GX Data Docs site configuration (S3 path, GCS bucket, etc.)
paramsSchema.name: data_docs_site_config

paramsSchema.description: Quality dimensions to include: completeness, uniqueness, accuracy, timeliness, consistency
paramsSchema.name: scorecard_dimensions

paramsSchema.description: How to compute the overall score
paramsSchema.name: scoring_model

paramsSchema.description: Whether to publish scorecard to data catalog
paramsSchema.name: publish_to_catalog

description: Runs a set of quality checks on your data: are required fields filled in? Are values in the expected range? Are there unexpected nulls? Catches data problems before they reach reports and dashboards.

inputPorts.description: Data to run quality checks against
inputPorts.name: data_to_validate

name: Data Quality Validator

paramsSchema.description: Array of GX expectation configs: {type, kwargs, severity}. Example type: ExpectColumnValuesToNotBeNull
paramsSchema.name: expectations

paramsSchema.description: Behavior on validation failure
paramsSchema.name: on_failure

paramsSchema.description: Minimum success percentage to pass
paramsSchema.name: threshold_percent

paramsSchema.description: Default GX mostly parameter (0.0-1.0) for all expectations
paramsSchema.name: mostly

paramsSchema.description: Pipeline-working-storage backend. Resolved at codegen and deploy time via storage_backends (tenant_id, environment, backend) lookup. Default inherits from pipeline.default_storage_backend when set.
paramsSchema.name: storage_backend

usageGuidance.when_to_use: Validate data quality with per-rule thresholds, quarantine support

description: Monitors whether your data is arriving on time. If the daily sales data usually shows up by 6am and it is 8am with nothing new, this alerts your team so they can investigate before it affects downstream reports.
name: Freshness Checks

paramsSchema.description: Column on the dataset that records ingestion or update time.
paramsSchema.name: timestamp_column

paramsSchema.description: Hard ceiling in calendar minutes. Use for sub-hour SLAs.
paramsSchema.name: max_age_minutes

paramsSchema.description: Hard ceiling in calendar hours. Use for daily SLAs that don't care about weekends.
paramsSchema.name: max_age_hours

paramsSchema.description: Hard ceiling in business days (skips weekends + holidays in the configured calendar). Use for batch SLAs that span the weekend.
paramsSchema.name: max_age_business_days

paramsSchema.description: Calendar for max_age_business_days computation.
paramsSchema.name: holiday_calendar_id

paramsSchema.description: Fiscal offset (unused for FreshnessChecks; included for resolver compatibility).
paramsSchema.name: fiscal_offset_months

paramsSchema.description: Pipeline-working-storage backend. Resolved at codegen and deploy time via storage_backends (tenant_id, environment, backend) lookup. Default inherits from pipeline.default_storage_backend when set.
paramsSchema.name: storage_backend

usageGuidance.when_to_use: Check source data freshness by timestamp column age

description: Direct access to the full Great Expectations catalog. Configure individual expectations with parameters, severity levels, and failure behavior. Runs GX Checkpoint inline in PySpark jobs with ephemeral context.

inputPorts.description: Spark DataFrame or table reference to validate
inputPorts.name: data_to_validate

name: GX Expectation Suite

paramsSchema.description: Array of GX expectations: {type, kwargs, severity}
paramsSchema.name: expectations

paramsSchema.description: Behavior when expectations fail
paramsSchema.name: on_failure

paramsSchema.description: Generate GX Data Docs HTML
paramsSchema.name: data_docs_enabled

paramsSchema.description: Slack webhook URL for failure notifications
paramsSchema.name: slack_webhook

paramsSchema.description: Email addresses for failure notifications
paramsSchema.name: email_recipients

description: Verifies that relationships between tables are valid -- every order references a real customer, every transaction references a real account. Catches broken links before they cause errors in reports.
name: Referential Integrity Check

paramsSchema.description: Foreign key column in child table
paramsSchema.name: child_key_column

paramsSchema.description: Primary key column in parent table
paramsSchema.name: parent_key_column

paramsSchema.description: Whether null FK values are allowed
paramsSchema.name: allow_nulls

paramsSchema.description: How to handle integrity violations
paramsSchema.name: violation_policy

paramsSchema.description: Mapped GX expectation for referential integrity
paramsSchema.name: gx_expectation

description: Watches for unexpected changes in your data structure -- like a column being renamed, removed, or having its type changed upstream. Catches these surprises before they silently break your pipelines.

inputPorts.description: New data to check for schema changes
inputPorts.name: incoming_data

name: Schema Drift Detection

paramsSchema.description: [{name, type}]
paramsSchema.name: expected_columns

paramsSchema.description: Pipeline-working-storage backend. Resolved at codegen and deploy time via storage_backends (tenant_id, environment, backend) lookup. Default inherits from pipeline.default_storage_backend when set.
paramsSchema.name: storage_backend

usageGuidance.when_to_use: Detect schema changes between runs

description: Compares data between two systems (like source database vs. data lake) to make sure nothing was lost or changed during transfer. Counts rows, sums amounts, and reports any mismatches.
name: Source-Target Reconciliation

paramsSchema.description: Type of reconciliation comparison
paramsSchema.name: reconciliation_type

paramsSchema.description: Columns to match records between source and target
paramsSchema.name: key_columns

paramsSchema.description: Columns to compare values on (for column_sum and full_match)
paramsSchema.name: value_columns

paramsSchema.description: Acceptable difference percentage
paramsSchema.name: tolerance_percent

paramsSchema.description: Mapped GX expectations: ExpectTableRowCountToEqualOtherTable, custom reconciliation via paired validation
paramsSchema.name: gx_expectations

description: Writes data to a relational database like PostgreSQL or Databricks via JDBC. Supports batch inserts, upserts, and configurable connection pooling for reliable delivery.

inputPorts.description: Data to write to the database
inputPorts.name: data_input

name: Database Writer

paramsSchema.description: Sink target SoR id. Reference to SystemOfRecord with metadata.registry_type='TARGET', listed at /api/v1/tenants/{tenantId}/targets.
paramsSchema.name: target_id

paramsSchema.description: ConnectorInstance attached to the chosen target. Listed at /api/v1/sors/{target_id}/connectors. Drives the __DEST_NAME__ credential prefix at codegen time.
paramsSchema.name: connector_instance_id

paramsSchema.description: Display name of the chosen connector instance. Auto-populated by the sink-add wizard; agent path may leave blank.
paramsSchema.name: connector_name

paramsSchema.description: Optional gcp-sm:// reference to override the connector instance's default credential binding. Resolved at runtime by the secret manager.
paramsSchema.name: target_credential_ref

paramsSchema.description: Fully-qualified destination table, e.g. analytics.fct_orders.
paramsSchema.name: target_table

paramsSchema.description: overwrite_partition deletes today's partition then inserts (idempotent on re-run). merge_on_pk uses INSERT ... ON CONFLICT keyed on upsert_keys.
paramsSchema.name: write_mode

paramsSchema.description: Required when write_mode=merge_on_pk.
paramsSchema.name: upsert_keys

paramsSchema.description: JDBC batchsize. 5000 is the practical lower bound for Postgres; smaller and round-trip overhead dominates.
paramsSchema.name: batch_size

paramsSchema.description: Destination partition columns (must already exist on the destination table).
paramsSchema.name: partition_by

usageGuidance.when_to_use: Write to PostgreSQL or Databricks

description: Writes data to a data lake in formats like Delta Lake, Apache Iceberg, or Parquet on S3 and other object stores. Supports partitioning, schema evolution, and compaction options.

inputPorts.description: Data to write to the data lake
inputPorts.name: data_input

name: Lake Writer

paramsSchema.description: Sink target SoR id (the consuming bucket / lake registry).
paramsSchema.name: target_id

paramsSchema.description: ConnectorInstance attached to the chosen target. Drives __DEST_NAME__ credential prefix and S3/GCS/Iceberg-catalog wiring.
paramsSchema.name: connector_instance_id

paramsSchema.description: Display name of the chosen connector instance.
paramsSchema.name: connector_name

paramsSchema.description: Optional gcp-sm:// override for the connector instance's default credentials.
paramsSchema.name: target_credential_ref

paramsSchema.description: Selects which codegen variant emits — sinks/lake_delta.py vs sinks/lake_iceberg.py.
paramsSchema.name: lake_format

paramsSchema.description: Destination path (s3a://... for Delta, catalog.namespace.table for Iceberg).
paramsSchema.name: output_path

paramsSchema.description: merge_on_pk uses DeltaTable.merge / Iceberg MERGE INTO. append_partition uses replaceWhere on ds (idempotent).
paramsSchema.name: write_mode

paramsSchema.description: Required when write_mode=merge_on_pk.
paramsSchema.name: merge_keys

paramsSchema.description: Run OPTIMIZE + Z-ORDER after the write. Adds 30s-2m runtime; bounds query latency as files accumulate.
paramsSchema.name: optimize_after_write

paramsSchema.description: High-cardinality filter columns for Delta z-ordering. Required when optimize_after_write=true.
paramsSchema.name: z_order_columns

usageGuidance.when_to_use: Write to object storage in Delta, Iceberg, or Parquet format

description: Publishes data to a streaming platform like Apache Kafka. Supports configurable serialization, partitioning, and delivery guarantees for real-time data pipelines.

inputPorts.description: Data to publish to the stream
inputPorts.name: data_input

name: Stream Writer

paramsSchema.description: Sink target SoR id (the Kafka cluster registry entry).
paramsSchema.name: target_id

paramsSchema.description: ConnectorInstance attached to the chosen target. Drives __DEST_NAME__ credential prefix (BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS, SCHEMA_REGISTRY_URL, KAFKA_USERNAME, KAFKA_PASSWORD).
paramsSchema.name: connector_instance_id

paramsSchema.description: Display name of the chosen connector instance.
paramsSchema.name: connector_name

paramsSchema.description: Optional gcp-sm:// override for the connector instance's default credentials.
paramsSchema.name: target_credential_ref

paramsSchema.description: Destination Kafka topic. Topic ACLs must be configured at the target connector level.
paramsSchema.name: topic

paramsSchema.description: batch_publish reads today's gold partition once. streaming_publish reads Delta CDF and forwards continuously.
paramsSchema.name: publish_mode

paramsSchema.description: Wire format. avro_schema_registry requires SCHEMA_REGISTRY_URL on the connector instance.
paramsSchema.name: schema_strategy

paramsSchema.description: Business-key columns hashed (SHA256) into the Kafka key, guaranteeing same-key rows land on same partition for ordering.
paramsSchema.name: key_columns

paramsSchema.description: exactly_once requires producer transactions + idempotent downstream consumer.
paramsSchema.name: delivery_guarantee

paramsSchema.description: Required for streaming_publish. s3a://... path; MUST be unique per pipeline (sharing checkpoints silently corrupts state).
paramsSchema.name: checkpoint_location

usageGuidance.when_to_use: Publish to Kafka topic

description: Writes data to a cloud data warehouse like Snowflake, BigQuery, or Redshift. Supports full-load and incremental write modes with options for merge keys and write disposition.

inputPorts.description: Data to write to the warehouse
inputPorts.name: data_input

name: Warehouse Writer

paramsSchema.description: Sink target SoR id (the warehouse registry entry).
paramsSchema.name: target_id

paramsSchema.description: ConnectorInstance attached to the chosen target. Drives __DEST_NAME__ credential prefix and warehouse-specific options (TEMP_GCS_BUCKET for BigQuery, etc.).
paramsSchema.name: connector_instance_id

paramsSchema.description: Display name of the chosen connector instance.
paramsSchema.name: connector_name

paramsSchema.description: Optional gcp-sm:// override (e.g. service-account JSON). Required when connector instance does not have a workload-identity binding.
paramsSchema.name: target_credential_ref

paramsSchema.description: Fully-qualified destination, e.g. project.dataset.table for BigQuery, database.schema.table for Snowflake.
paramsSchema.name: target_table

paramsSchema.description: overwrite_partition replaces the matched partition (datePartition for BQ). merge_on_pk stages then issues MERGE.
paramsSchema.name: write_mode

paramsSchema.description: Required when write_mode=merge_on_pk.
paramsSchema.name: merge_keys

paramsSchema.description: Avro batch size for the indirect write method.
paramsSchema.name: batch_size

paramsSchema.description: Destination clustering columns (must already exist on the destination table). Critical for merge_on_pk runtime.
paramsSchema.name: clustering_columns

usageGuidance.when_to_use: Write to BigQuery, Snowflake, or Redshift

description: Connects to a REST API (like Salesforce, Stripe, or any web service), pulls data page by page, and loads it into your data lake. Respects rate limits so you do not get blocked by the source system.
name: API Ingestion

outputPorts.description: API response data landed in the data lake
outputPorts.name: api_output

paramsSchema.description: REST endpoint. May contain Airflow-style {{ ds }} templating for date-driven endpoints.
paramsSchema.name: api_url

paramsSchema.description: Authentication scheme.
paramsSchema.name: auth_type

paramsSchema.description: gcp-sm:// reference to the credential. Resolved at runtime by the secret manager. Required for any auth_type other than none.
paramsSchema.name: auth_credential_ref

paramsSchema.description: Max requests per minute. Client throttles to stay under this.
paramsSchema.name: rate_limit_rpm

paramsSchema.description: Column / response field that drives incremental ingestion (the watermark column). Empty = full pull each run.
paramsSchema.name: incremental_field

paramsSchema.description: JSONPath to the rows array within the response payload.
paramsSchema.name: response_json_path

paramsSchema.description: Bounded retries on 5xx / network error before failing the task.
paramsSchema.name: retry_count

paramsSchema.description: Per-request timeout.
paramsSchema.name: timeout_seconds

paramsSchema.description: Pipeline-working-storage backend. Resolved at codegen and deploy time via storage_backends (tenant_id, environment, backend) lookup. Default inherits from pipeline.default_storage_backend when set.
paramsSchema.name: storage_backend

paramsSchema.description: Medallion layer of the table this blueprint produces. Ingestion blueprints typically land at bronze; agent should not change without explicit user direction.
paramsSchema.name: lake_layer

paramsSchema.description: Physical table format. Legal options depend on (storage_backend, lake_layer): DPC supports delta|iceberg_external|parquet; GCP non-gold supports delta|iceberg_external|iceberg_bq_managed; GCP gold MUST be bq_native (locked rule, DB-enforced).
paramsSchema.name: lake_format

usageGuidance.when_to_use: Pull data from REST APIs with pagination and auth

description: Loads a large volume of historical data in one go -- for example, importing three years of transactions when setting up a new pipeline. Runs in parallel chunks so it finishes in hours instead of days.
name: Bulk Backfill

paramsSchema.description: JDBC SELECT to source rows. Should reference the date column for predicate pushdown.
paramsSchema.name: source_query

paramsSchema.description: First date of the backfill window. Accepts ISO date or PULSE mnemonic (e.g., BOM-12, BOY-1, 2024-01-01).
paramsSchema.name: date_range_start

paramsSchema.description: Last date of the backfill window. Accepts ISO date or PULSE mnemonic (e.g., EOM-1, T-1, EOQ).
paramsSchema.name: date_range_end

paramsSchema.description: Rows per JDBC fetch chunk. Tune to source row size.
paramsSchema.name: chunk_size

paramsSchema.description: Calendar days per processing chunk. Smaller = finer-grained idempotency, more roundtrips. Default ~1 month is balanced.
paramsSchema.name: chunk_days

paramsSchema.description: JDBC partition count for parallel reads.
paramsSchema.name: parallelism

paramsSchema.description: Holiday calendar for business-day mnemonic resolution.
paramsSchema.name: holiday_calendar_id

paramsSchema.description: Months between fiscal year start and calendar year start.
paramsSchema.name: fiscal_offset_months

paramsSchema.description: Pipeline-working-storage backend. Resolved at codegen and deploy time via storage_backends (tenant_id, environment, backend) lookup. Default inherits from pipeline.default_storage_backend when set.
paramsSchema.name: storage_backend

paramsSchema.description: Medallion layer of the table this blueprint produces. Ingestion blueprints typically land at bronze; agent should not change without explicit user direction.
paramsSchema.name: lake_layer

paramsSchema.description: Physical table format. Legal options depend on (storage_backend, lake_layer): DPC supports delta|iceberg_external|parquet; GCP non-gold supports delta|iceberg_external|iceberg_bq_managed; GCP gold MUST be bq_native (locked rule, DB-enforced).
paramsSchema.name: lake_format

usageGuidance.when_to_use: Historical backfill over a date range

description: Watches a database for changes (new rows, updates, deletes) and streams only the changes into your data lake. Like having a live feed of everything happening in the source system without copying the whole database every time.
name: Change Data Capture Ingestion

paramsSchema.description: Required when cdc_mode=incremental_poll
paramsSchema.name: incremental_column

paramsSchema.description: Pipeline-working-storage backend. Resolved at codegen and deploy time via storage_backends (tenant_id, environment, backend) lookup. Default inherits from pipeline.default_storage_backend when set.
paramsSchema.name: storage_backend

paramsSchema.description: Medallion layer of the table this blueprint produces. Ingestion blueprints typically land at bronze; agent should not change without explicit user direction.
paramsSchema.name: lake_layer

paramsSchema.description: Physical table format. Legal options depend on (storage_backend, lake_layer): DPC supports delta|iceberg_external|parquet; GCP non-gold supports delta|iceberg_external|iceberg_bq_managed; GCP gold MUST be bq_native (locked rule, DB-enforced).
paramsSchema.name: lake_format

usageGuidance.when_to_use: True CDC via Debezium or incremental polling fallback

description: Picks up data files (CSV, JSON, Parquet, Excel) from a folder or cloud storage bucket and loads them into your data lake. Handles large files in batches and organizes them by date so nothing gets lost.
name: File Ingestion

outputPorts.description: Raw ingested data in the data lake
outputPorts.name: raw_output

paramsSchema.description: Fields inherited from the linked connector instance. Read-only at this surface; configure on the connector. Typically: bucket, path_prefix, auth, region.
paramsSchema.name: _inherited_from_connector

paramsSchema.description: Filename pattern at the connector path. With pattern_kind=template, use {date} placeholder (e.g., loan_master_{date}.csv). With glob, shell wildcards. With regex, Java regex.
paramsSchema.name: filename_pattern

paramsSchema.description: Java date format for {date} substitution when pattern_kind=template.
paramsSchema.name: date_format

paramsSchema.description: Date for {date} substitution. PULSE mnemonic (PBD = previous business day, RUN_DATE = today, T-1 = yesterday) or ISO date.
paramsSchema.name: date_value

paramsSchema.description: CSV delimiter; ignored for non-CSV formats.
paramsSchema.name: delimiter

paramsSchema.description: Skip the header row from the file.
paramsSchema.name: has_header

paramsSchema.description: Bronze partition columns (typically ["ds"]).
paramsSchema.name: partition_by

paramsSchema.description: Minimum file size in bytes to accept; 0 disables. Guards against partial uploads.
paramsSchema.name: expected_size_min

paramsSchema.description: Pipeline-working-storage backend. Resolved at codegen and deploy time via storage_backends (tenant_id, environment, backend) lookup. Default inherits from pipeline.default_storage_backend when set.
paramsSchema.name: storage_backend

paramsSchema.description: Medallion layer of the table this blueprint produces. Ingestion blueprints typically land at bronze; agent should not change without explicit user direction.
paramsSchema.name: lake_layer

paramsSchema.description: Physical table format. Legal options depend on (storage_backend, lake_layer): DPC supports delta|iceberg_external|parquet; GCP non-gold supports delta|iceberg_external|iceberg_bq_managed; GCP gold MUST be bq_native (locked rule, DB-enforced).
paramsSchema.name: lake_format

usageGuidance.when_to_use: Ingest files from S3, GCS, SFTP, or local filesystem

description: Takes a complete picture of a data source at a point in time -- like a photograph of the entire table. Useful for systems that do not track changes, so you compare snapshots to figure out what changed.
name: Snapshot Ingestion

paramsSchema.description: Inherited from connector
paramsSchema.name: source_table

paramsSchema.description: Pipeline-working-storage backend. Resolved at codegen and deploy time via storage_backends (tenant_id, environment, backend) lookup. Default inherits from pipeline.default_storage_backend when set.
paramsSchema.name: storage_backend

paramsSchema.description: Medallion layer of the table this blueprint produces. Ingestion blueprints typically land at bronze; agent should not change without explicit user direction.
paramsSchema.name: lake_layer

paramsSchema.description: Physical table format. Legal options depend on (storage_backend, lake_layer): DPC supports delta|iceberg_external|parquet; GCP non-gold supports delta|iceberg_external|iceberg_bq_managed; GCP gold MUST be bq_native (locked rule, DB-enforced).
paramsSchema.name: lake_format

paramsSchema.description: Medallion layer of the table this blueprint produces. Ingestion blueprints typically land at bronze; agent should not change without explicit user direction.
paramsSchema.name: lake_layer

paramsSchema.description: Physical table format. Legal options depend on (storage_backend, lake_layer): DPC supports delta|iceberg_external|parquet; GCP non-gold supports delta|iceberg_external|iceberg_bq_managed; GCP gold MUST be bq_native (locked rule, DB-enforced).
paramsSchema.name: lake_format

description: Builds a dimension table that keeps the full history of changes -- so you can see what a customer's address was last year, not just today. Every change creates a new version of the record with date ranges.
name: SCD2 Dimension

outputPorts.description: Slowly changing dimension with full history
outputPorts.name: scd2_output

paramsSchema.description: Columns that trigger a new version when changed
paramsSchema.name: tracked_columns

paramsSchema.description: Columns to partition by
paramsSchema.name: partition_by

paramsSchema.description: Columns to cluster by (BigQuery)
paramsSchema.name: cluster_by

paramsSchema.description: Pipeline-working-storage backend. Resolved at codegen and deploy time via storage_backends (tenant_id, environment, backend) lookup. Default inherits from pipeline.default_storage_backend when set.
paramsSchema.name: storage_backend

paramsSchema.description: Medallion layer of the table this blueprint produces. Modeling blueprints typically land at gold.

paramsSchema.name: lake_format

usageGuidance.when_to_use: Track full history for mutable master data (employees, customers) via SCD2 snapshot. NO data quality rules — for DQ use DQValidator (GX).

description: Structures time-stamped data (sensor readings, stock prices, daily metrics) for fast time-based queries. Applies partitioning, indexing, and optional downsampling so your time series analysis runs efficiently.
name: Time Series Optimization

description: Advances the business as-of date/time of a dataset to the next interval based on its configured time grain. Place this at the end of a pipeline to automatically move the dataset forward after successful processing. Can also advance the domain-level business date.

inputPorts.description: Connect to the last processing step so advance happens after data is ready
inputPorts.name: trigger

name: Advance Time Dimension

outputPorts.description: Advance result with previous and new as-of values
outputPorts.name: status

paramsSchema.description: Pipeline-working-storage backend. Resolved at codegen and deploy time via storage_backends (tenant_id, environment, backend) lookup. Default inherits from pipeline.default_storage_backend when set.
paramsSchema.name: storage_backend

usageGuidance.when_to_use: Advance dataset or domain business date after successful run

description: Re-runs your pipeline for a specific date range in the past. Useful when you fix a bug and need to reprocess last month's data, or when you add a new pipeline and need to catch up on historical data.
name: Backfill & Replay

paramsSchema.description: First date of the replay window. Accepts ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) or PULSE mnemonic (e.g., BOM-12, BOY, T-365). Resolved at runtime against PULSE_BUSINESS_DATE.
paramsSchema.name: start_date

paramsSchema.description: Last date of the replay window. Accepts ISO date or PULSE mnemonic (e.g., EOM-1, T-1, EOQ).
paramsSchema.name: end_date

paramsSchema.description: Concurrent chunks. Higher values speed replay but increase load on the source.
paramsSchema.name: parallelism

paramsSchema.description: If true, delete existing rows in the window before re-ingesting. If false (default), use replaceWhere idempotency.
paramsSchema.name: clear_existing

paramsSchema.description: Holiday calendar for business-day mnemonic resolution. Inherited from domain.business_date_config when not set.
paramsSchema.name: holiday_calendar_id

paramsSchema.description: Months between fiscal year start and calendar year start (0 = aligned, 9 = US fiscal Oct 1 start). Inherited from domain.business_date_config when not set.
paramsSchema.name: fiscal_offset_months

paramsSchema.description: Pipeline-working-storage backend. Resolved at codegen and deploy time via storage_backends (tenant_id, environment, backend) lookup. Default inherits from pipeline.default_storage_backend when set.
paramsSchema.name: storage_backend

usageGuidance.when_to_use: Configure historical backfill with date range and parallelism

description: Tracks how much compute and storage each pipeline run costs. Alerts your team when spending exceeds budgets or when a pipeline suddenly gets much more expensive -- prevents surprise cloud bills.
name: Cost Monitoring Hook

paramsSchema.description: Pipeline-working-storage backend. Resolved at codegen and deploy time via storage_backends (tenant_id, environment, backend) lookup. Default inherits from pipeline.default_storage_backend when set.
paramsSchema.name: storage_backend

usageGuidance.when_to_use: Set daily budget limits and compute/storage tracking

description: Runs a polling SQL check until the source or dependency is ready.
name: Database Readiness Sensor

outputPorts.description: Proceed when database readiness check passes
outputPorts.name: ready_signal

paramsSchema.description: Probe SQL. May contain {date} placeholder (substituted via date_value+date_format) for date-relative readiness checks. Example: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM landing WHERE ds = '{date}'.
paramsSchema.name: sql

paramsSchema.description: Airflow connection_id pointing at the source DB.
paramsSchema.name: connection_id

paramsSchema.description: Java date format for {date} substitution in sql.
paramsSchema.name: date_format

paramsSchema.description: Date for {date} substitution. PULSE mnemonic or ISO date.
paramsSchema.name: date_value

paramsSchema.description: Minimum row count to consider source ready. Default 1 (any rows).
paramsSchema.name: expected_count_min

paramsSchema.description: Optional ceiling. Use to detect runaway loads (e.g., 10x normal volume signals upstream defect).
paramsSchema.name: expected_count_max

paramsSchema.description: Pipeline-working-storage backend. Resolved at codegen and deploy time via storage_backends (tenant_id, environment, backend) lookup. Default inherits from pipeline.default_storage_backend when set.
paramsSchema.name: storage_backend

description: Waits for another DAG or task dependency before continuing.
name: Dataset Dependency Sensor

outputPorts.description: Proceed when upstream dependency is satisfied
outputPorts.name: ready_signal

paramsSchema.description: Pipeline-working-storage backend. Resolved at codegen and deploy time via storage_backends (tenant_id, environment, backend) lookup. Default inherits from pipeline.default_storage_backend when set.
paramsSchema.name: storage_backend

description: Polls an HTTP endpoint or event source until an external trigger is observed.
name: External Event Sensor

outputPorts.description: Proceed when external event is observed
outputPorts.name: ready_signal

paramsSchema.description: Pipeline-working-storage backend. Resolved at codegen and deploy time via storage_backends (tenant_id, environment, backend) lookup. Default inherits from pipeline.default_storage_backend when set.
paramsSchema.name: storage_backend

description: Waits for a file path or prefix to become available before continuing the pipeline.
name: File Arrival Sensor

outputPorts.description: Proceed when file arrival is confirmed
outputPorts.name: ready_signal

paramsSchema.description: Object-storage backend hosting the file. s3 covers MinIO and AWS S3. gcs covers Google Cloud Storage. sftp covers SFTP servers (no bucket; the bucket field becomes the SFTP host).
paramsSchema.name: storage_kind

paramsSchema.description: Bucket name (S3/GCS) or SFTP host. PULSE convention: {tenant_slug}_{domain_slug} (e.g., home-lending_servicing). Derived from tenant + domain context if left blank.
paramsSchema.name: bucket

paramsSchema.description: Path prefix under the bucket (e.g., inbound/msp/). Trailing slash optional; PULSE normalizes.
paramsSchema.name: path_prefix

paramsSchema.description: Filename pattern. For pattern_kind=template, use {date} as the date placeholder (e.g., loan_master_{date}.csv). For glob, use shell-style wildcards (e.g., *.csv). For regex, a Java regex (e.g., ^loan_master_\\d{8}\\.csv$).
paramsSchema.name: filename_pattern

paramsSchema.description: How to interpret filename_pattern. template substitutes {date} via date_value+date_format. glob matches shell-style wildcards. regex matches a Java regular expression.
paramsSchema.name: pattern_kind

paramsSchema.description: Java date format used to substitute {date} in filename_pattern when pattern_kind=template. Common values: yyyyMMdd, yyyy-MM-dd, MMddyyyy.
paramsSchema.name: date_format

paramsSchema.description: Date used to substitute {date} in filename_pattern. PULSE mnemonic (PBD = previous business day; RUN_DATE = today; T-1 = yesterday; BOM = beginning of month) or ISO date. Resolved at task execution time against PULSE_BUSINESS_DATE.
paramsSchema.name: date_value

paramsSchema.description: Minimum file size in bytes to count as arrived. 0 disables the check. Use to guard against partial uploads.
paramsSchema.name: expected_size_min

paramsSchema.description: Optional stale-file guard. If the matched file is older than this many hours, treat as not-arrived.
paramsSchema.name: expected_max_age_hours

paramsSchema.description: If true, accept any matching file (glob-like multi-file landing). If false (default), succeed when exactly one file matches.
paramsSchema.name: multiple_files_mode

paramsSchema.description: If true, sensor timeout marks the task SKIPPED (not FAILED), allowing the rest of the DAG to run. Almost always false for ingestion sensors.
paramsSchema.name: soft_fail

paramsSchema.description: Seconds between checks. 60 for sub-minute landings, 300 for minute-scale, 3600 for hour-scale.
paramsSchema.name: poke_interval_seconds

paramsSchema.description: Hard fail after this many seconds. Default 4h.
paramsSchema.name: timeout_seconds

paramsSchema.description: reschedule frees the worker slot between checks (recommended for long waits). poke holds the slot (for fast polling).
paramsSchema.name: mode

paramsSchema.description: Holiday calendar for resolving date_value mnemonics like PBD.
paramsSchema.name: holiday_calendar_id

paramsSchema.description: Months between fiscal year and calendar year. Used by fiscal mnemonics (BOFY, EOFQ, etc.) in date_value.
paramsSchema.name: fiscal_offset_months

paramsSchema.description: Pipeline-working-storage backend. Resolved at codegen and deploy time via storage_backends (tenant_id, environment, backend) lookup. Default inherits from pipeline.default_storage_backend when set.
paramsSchema.name: storage_backend

description: Waits for an object-store key or prefix to become available before continuing the pipeline.
name: Object Store Key Sensor

outputPorts.description: Proceed when object-store readiness is confirmed
outputPorts.name: ready_signal

paramsSchema.description: Object-storage backend (s3 also covers MinIO).
paramsSchema.name: storage_kind

paramsSchema.description: Bucket name. PULSE convention: {tenant_slug}_{domain_slug}.
paramsSchema.name: bucket

paramsSchema.description: Path prefix under the bucket. Optional for top-level keys.
paramsSchema.name: key_prefix

paramsSchema.description: Object key pattern. With pattern_kind=template, use {date} for date substitution (e.g., daily_export_{date}/_SUCCESS). With glob, shell wildcards. With regex, Java regex.
paramsSchema.name: key_pattern

paramsSchema.description: Interpretation of key_pattern.
paramsSchema.name: pattern_kind

paramsSchema.description: Java date format for {date} substitution.
paramsSchema.name: date_format

paramsSchema.description: Date for {date} substitution. PULSE mnemonic or ISO date.
paramsSchema.name: date_value

paramsSchema.description: Minimum object size in bytes.
paramsSchema.name: expected_size_min

paramsSchema.description: If true, succeed when ANY matching object exists. If false, succeed when exactly one matches.
paramsSchema.name: multiple_files_mode

paramsSchema.description: On timeout: SKIPPED (true) vs FAILED (false).
paramsSchema.name: soft_fail

paramsSchema.description: Holiday calendar for date_value mnemonic resolution.
paramsSchema.name: holiday_calendar_id

paramsSchema.description: Fiscal year offset for mnemonic resolution.
paramsSchema.name: fiscal_offset_months

paramsSchema.description: Pipeline-working-storage backend. Resolved at codegen and deploy time via storage_backends (tenant_id, environment, backend) lookup. Default inherits from pipeline.default_storage_backend when set.
paramsSchema.name: storage_backend

description: Automatically reverts to the last working version if a deployment fails or causes errors. Like an undo button for deployments -- your pipeline keeps running on the known-good version while you fix the problem.
name: Rollback on Failure

paramsSchema.description: Pipeline-working-storage backend. Resolved at codegen and deploy time via storage_backends (tenant_id, environment, backend) lookup. Default inherits from pipeline.default_storage_backend when set.
paramsSchema.name: storage_backend

usageGuidance.when_to_use: Define rollback behavior on deploy or health-check failure

description: Defines when and how your pipeline runs -- on a schedule (every day at 6am), on a trigger (when new data arrives), or on demand. The control panel for your pipeline's timing.
name: Schedule & Triggers

paramsSchema.description: Standard 5-field cron (e.g., 0 6 * * 1-5 = 6am Mon-Fri). Required when schedule_type=cron.
paramsSchema.name: cron_expression

paramsSchema.description: Dataset whose update triggers this pipeline. Required when schedule_type=event.
paramsSchema.name: trigger_dataset

paramsSchema.description: IANA timezone for the cron expression (e.g., America/New_York).
paramsSchema.name: timezone

paramsSchema.description: Max concurrent DAG runs. 1 prevents overlap (recommended for SCD2 / ordering-sensitive pipelines).
paramsSchema.name: max_active_runs

paramsSchema.description: If true, on first deploy Airflow schedules a run for every interval from start_date to today. DEFAULT FALSE — only enable when historical backfill is explicitly desired (per #28 locked rule).
paramsSchema.name: catchup_enabled

paramsSchema.description: If true, today's run waits for yesterday's run to succeed. Use for incremental pipelines where today's output depends on yesterday's state.
paramsSchema.name: depends_on_past

paramsSchema.description: Per-task retries before the DAG fails.
paramsSchema.name: retry_count

paramsSchema.description: Pipeline-working-storage backend. Resolved at codegen and deploy time via storage_backends (tenant_id, environment, backend) lookup. Default inherits from pipeline.default_storage_backend when set.
paramsSchema.name: storage_backend

usageGuidance.when_to_use: Define pipeline schedule: cron, event-triggered, or manual

description: Takes raw, messy data and cleans it up: fixes data types, removes junk rows, standardizes formats (like dates and phone numbers), and produces a reliable, query-ready version of the data.
name: Bronze-to-Silver Cleaning

paramsSchema.description: Map of {column: target_type} casts. Example: {"created_at": "timestamp", "amount": "decimal(18,2)"}.
paramsSchema.name: type_coercions

paramsSchema.description: String columns to trim leading/trailing whitespace.
paramsSchema.name: columns_to_trim

paramsSchema.description: Map of {old_column_name: new_column_name}. Renames must be reflected in downstream contracts.
paramsSchema.name: rename_map

paramsSchema.description: Map of {column: action} where action is "keep" / "coerce_to:<value>" / "drop_row". Default: keep.
paramsSchema.name: null_handling

paramsSchema.description: Optional dedup-on-key (uses ROW_NUMBER over the key, keeps latest). For full-fledged dedup use the DedupeAndMerge blueprint.
paramsSchema.name: dedup_key

paramsSchema.description: Columns to omit from silver output.
paramsSchema.name: drop_columns

paramsSchema.description: Silver partition columns. Default partitions by ds (the bronze ingest date).
paramsSchema.name: partition_by

description: Reshapes your data to fit your company official data model -- the single agreed-upon way data should be structured. Can also standardize dimension values (regions, products, cost centers) using lookup tables so everything is consistent across reports.

paramsSchema.name: dimension_lookups

description: Organizes data using the Data Vault methodology -- a proven approach for building a flexible, auditable data warehouse. Separates business keys, relationships, and descriptive details into modular building blocks.

inputPorts.description: Cleaned data ready for Data Vault loading
inputPorts.name: staged_data

name: Data Vault Hub-Link-Satellite

description: Finds and removes duplicate records -- like when the same customer appears three times with slightly different spellings. Merges the best information from each duplicate into one golden record.
name: Dedupe & Merge

paramsSchema.description: Business-key columns that define the dedup partition. ROW_NUMBER partitions by these.
paramsSchema.name: match_keys

paramsSchema.description: Ordered ranking columns: [{column, direction}]. Direction = "asc" | "desc". REQUIRED to be deterministic — include a tiebreaker (typically the surrogate key) at the end so re-runs produce identical output.
paramsSchema.name: order_by_columns

paramsSchema.description: exact = string equality. fuzzy = soundex / Levenshtein (for name dedup). composite = AND across multiple match_keys.
paramsSchema.name: match_strategy

paramsSchema.description: Optional explicit priority column when ordering ties. If unset, the last column of order_by_columns acts as the tiebreaker.
paramsSchema.name: merge_priority

paramsSchema.description: Silver partition columns.
paramsSchema.name: partition_by

paramsSchema.description: row_number = portable Spark idiom. qualify = compact dialect-specific (Snowflake/Spark). distinct_on = Postgres-only.
paramsSchema.name: dedup_method
